title: Multimodal generative AI for medical image interpretation

data_scope.paper_type: Perspective / Review — no new primary dataset
data_scope.key_benchmark: MIMIC-CXR (~227,835 imaging studies; figure from primary dataset paper, not stated in this article)
data_scope.data_availability: No new data released; MIMIC-CXR and RadGraph publicly available via PhysioNet

core_conclusion: Multimodal generative medical image interpretation (GenMI) — AI that reads medical images and writes narrative diagnostic reports — is advancing rapidly and early evidence suggests it can approach human expert performance in radiology, pathology, and dermatology. Critical barriers remain in validating accuracy at scale, ensuring transparency, and capturing nuanced clinical impressions. The authors propose a clinician-empowerment 'AI resident' paradigm as the safest near-term deployment model.
why_it_matters: Addresses a global radiologist shortage bottleneck; synthesizes a fast-moving, fragmented literature into a coherent capability taxonomy; proposes concrete deployment recommendations beyond benchmarks; extends beyond chest X-rays to CT, MRI, pathology, and dermatology.
why_nature: Broad societal impact across every healthcare system; cross-disciplinary convergence of computer vision, NLP, clinical medicine, and ethics; timely paradigm-defining Perspective for the GPT-4V era; responsible-AI framing aligned with Nature editorial values.
added_value_to_my_research: GenMI-augmented radiologist capacity as a new supply unit in 2SFCA/R2SFCA spatial accessibility models for healthcare
Causal deconfounding for demographic bias in GenMI mirrors spatial deconfounding in MGWR for health geography
Pixel-level explainability (grounded report generation) as the medical analogue of spatial SHAP over rasters
Longitudinal imaging-sequence modeling shares temporal kernel density / trajectory methods from spatiotemporal GeoAI
Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (LoRA/adapter) for geospatial foundation models directly transfers to specialty-scarce GenMI deployment

figures.caption: Applications for automated medical report generation — maps clinical use cases for GenMI across radiology, pathology, and dermatology
The capabilities of GenMI — taxonomy of model inputs, outputs, and architectural approaches for generative medical image interpretation
Implementing an AI resident — the proposed human-in-the-loop deployment paradigm mapping GenMI confidence levels to graded clinician oversight
